## v1.2.4 — No-Surprise Diagnostics for Slack MCP Operators
This release removes diagnostic side effects and makes setup/auth triage deterministic across CLI, MCP tool output, and web mode.

### Improved
- `--status` is now read-only and deterministic; it no longer triggers Chrome extraction side effects.
- `--doctor` exit-code behavior is enforced and covered for missing credentials (`1`) and invalid credentials (`2`) in install-flow verification.
- Token-age handling now treats missing timestamps as unknown age instead of false critical warnings.
- Chrome extraction failures now surface explicit Apple Events remediation guidance.
- Health and status payloads are normalized across handlers/endpoints (`status`, `code`, `message`, `next_action`).

### Compatibility
- No MCP tool renames or removals.
- Existing CLI commands remain intact (`--setup`, `--status`, `--doctor`, `web`, `http`).

### Verify
```bash
npx -y @jtalk22/slack-mcp --version
npx -y @jtalk22/slack-mcp --doctor
npx -y @jtalk22/slack-mcp --setup
npx -y @jtalk22/slack-mcp --status
```

### Upgrade
```bash
npm i -g @jtalk22/slack-mcp@1.2.4
```

### Notes
- If you run claude.ai in a browser, continue using `npx -y @jtalk22/slack-mcp web`.
- macOS auto-extraction still requires Chrome with `View > Developer > Allow JavaScript from Apple Events` enabled.
